That’s the new craft.

tweets.text:
The California Supreme Court just made it even harder to keep repeat offenders off the street before trial.

This is how public safety collapses: not all at once, but ruling by ruling, loophole by loophole, until normal people are the only ones following the law.

Brooke brought accountability back to San Francisco, now California needs to catch up.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: The CA Supreme Court just issued a major new ruling (In re Kowalczyk) that mandates that “attainable” bail be given to everyone not accused of a violent crime - including drug dealers, repeat offenders, and armed felons. It essentially guarantees their release back onto the

tweets.text:
BIP-110 could be bullish for Bitcoin Ordinals.

The soft fork blocks new inscriptions for ~1 year. 

No new BTC NFTs get minted in that window.

But everything inscribed before stays onchain forever.

So the existing NFTs become the only supply, for as long as the fork stays in place.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: apparently bip110 will self-fork off around aug 7th, or fail to activate🍿 https://t.co/EyOOIxh5lz @bitmexresearch https://t.co/T6eATQyKQY

tweets.text:
- $AAOI at $12B
- $SIVE at $2B
- Foci at $2.8B
- Shunsin at $2B 

Usually the best risk/reward to me currently. Lot of my answers before like $AXTI already 10x’d, so different lineup this time. 

$AAOI due to absurd H1 2027 revenue projections from capacity ramp, doing everything from laser fab to assembly in America. 

$471M/month… that’s in 2027, the TAM increases exponentially in 2028.

$SIVE is also ramping absurdly high, 77% revenue pipeline growth of the entire company’s history to ~$799M

Primarily from photonics… in a single quarter. And they’re projecting 60% gross margins off that.

Foci - $NVDA / $TSM primarily FAU supplier and bottleneck for COUPE. Genuinely not sure how this is $2.8B.

BOM share for their passive components + FAU are massive in 2028. Just a bit early H1 2026.

Shunsin - Legit you see Foxconn get CPO/photonics related orders over and over for $NVDA and others.

Just nobody knows the packaging/testing gets done by Shunsin.

A lot of contracts are also under Shunsin’s subsidiary too.. so markets/algorithms don’t know what’s coming imo.

Runner up is $XFAB, they’ll probably be central to EU CHIPS act 2 for silicon photonics at ~$1.5B MC.

And of course SiC/GaN foundries should go brr with 800vdc push by Nvidia. 

Especially if they’re the only high volume one in United States per Dpt. Of Commerce. 

And it’s such a low price/book ratio so you’re kinda getting the company upside for free, while US Gov/EU Gov subsidize their capex.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: @aleabitoreddit What are your top 4 stocks to buy right now, at current market capitalizations and offering the best risk/reward, if you could only choose 4?

BOM share for their passive components + FAU are massive in 2028. Just a bit early H1 2026.

Shunsin - Legit you see Foxconn get CPO/photonics related orders over and over for $NVDA and others.

Just nobody knows the packaging/testing gets done by Shunsin.

A lot of contracts are also under Shunsin’s subsidiary too.. so markets/algorithms don’t know what’s coming imo.

Runner up is $XFAB, they’ll probably be central to EU CHIPS act 2 for silicon photonics at ~$1.5B MC.

And of course SiC/GaN foundries should go brr with 800vdc push by Nvidia. 

Especially if they’re the only high volume one in United States per Dpt. Of Commerce. 

And it’s such a low price/book ratio so you’re kinda getting the company upside for free, while US Gov/EU Gov subsidize their capex.

- $AAOI at $12B
- $SIVE at $2B
- Foci at $2.8B

Usually the best risk/reward to me currently. Lot of my answers before like $AXTI already 10x’d, so different lineup this time. 

$AAOI due to absurd H1 2027 revenue projections from capacity ramp, doing everything from laser fab to assembly in America. 

$471M/month… that’s in 2027, the TAM increases exponentially in 2028.

$SIVE is also ramping absurdly high, 77% revenue pipeline growth of the entire company’s history to ~$799M

Primarily from photonics… in a single quarter. And they’re projecting 60% gross margins off that.

Foci - $NVDA / $TSM primarily FAU supplier and bottleneck for COUPE. Genuinely not sure how this is $2.8B.

BOM share for their passive components + FAU are massive in 2028. Just a bit early H1 2026.

Shunsin - Legit you see Foxconn get CPO/photonics related orders over and over for $NVDA and others.

Just nobody knows the packaging/testing gets done by Shunsin.

A lot of contracts are also under Shunsin’s subsidiary too.. so markets/algorithms don’t know what’s coming imo.

Runner up is $XFAB, they’ll probably be central to EU CHIPS act 2 for silicon photonics at ~$1.5B MC.

And of course SiC/GaN foundries should go brr with 800vdc push by Nvidia. 

Especially if they’re the only high volume one in United States per Dpt. Of Commerce. 

And it’s such a low price/book ratio so you’re kinda getting the company upside for free, while US Gov/EU Gov subsidize their capex.

tweets.text:
I personally never read that, although it is a good post, but also they aren't even the same conclusions outside some similar terminology.
Yours is about consumer surplus (valid point btw, we missed that in our post), but it is not a part of what we are countin because investors and monetary policy dont care about consumer surplus
Yours is about inflation going down, ours is about the data / measurement being wrong so not registering that.
Ours suggests that AI could become a negative force on measured GDP, as things move from countable to uncountable.
Both are arguing that the data is insufficient (this is not unique to either of us), but ours actually has implications of how and where some of its finger prints will show up.

I would say we are both standing on the shoulders of the same literature. Which is Kuznets 1934, Nordhaus & Tobin (1972), Solow (1987), Boskin Commission (1996), Brynjolfsson & Collis (2019), Varian Economics of Free etc.

tweets.text: @Colasama This was always an expected and necessary first step on the long road toward enabling DeFi perps in the USA. It’s easy to underestimate the complexity of these issues, but you can see how much work CFTC had to do in its releases to get to this point. Much more work to be done.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
When Chairman Selig says the CFTC will approve crypto perps in the next month, I'm guessing this means perps offered on centralized exchanges only.

Right now, "perps" on CEXes are actually long-dated futures, a different product.

Approval for DeFi perps will likely take longer.
tweets.full_text: @Colasama This was always an expected and necessary first step on the long road toward enabling DeFi perps in the USA. tweets.text:
ironically this could be the reason google ultimately fails to be #1 across any AI platform

> sold a huge chunk of TPU capacity to anthropic which takes compute from training future gemini models

> gemini 3.1 and 3.5 flash are amazing models but failed to catch-up in coding (i do think this gets resolved at the harness layer)

> cloud business is struggling to keep up with aws rate of acceleration. 

 google's allocation of compute budget will be their most critical decision over the next 6 months. 

theres a reason anthropic cfo and openai's sarah friar spend >50% of time determining where compute goes
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Google is fighting every final boss at once:

OpenAI & Anthropic in models, Nvidia in chips, AWS & Microsoft in cloud, Meta in ads, Tesla in self-driving, Apple in phones and OS.

At $4.6T, it feels weirdly undervalued.
